meta.description: Structured index of valve guideline recommendations for The Valve Wire editorial engine

sources.title: 2020 ACC/AHA Guideline for Management of Patients With Valvular Heart Disease
sources.citation: Otto CM et al. J Am Coll Cardiol. 2021;77:e25-197

sources.title: 2025 ESC/EACTS Guidelines for the Management of Valvular Heart Disease
sources.citation: Praz F, Borger MA et al. Eur Heart J. 2025;46:4635-4736

age_cutoffs.under_65: SAVR recommended (Class I, LOE A). No RCT data for TAVI in this group.
age_cutoffs.65_to_80: Either SAVR or transfemoral TAVI recommended after shared decision-making (Class I, LOE A). Balance longevity vs valve durability.
age_cutoffs.over_80: Transfemoral TAVI recommended in preference to SAVR if no anatomic contraindication (Class I, LOE A).
age_cutoffs.life_expectancy_less_10y: Transfemoral TAVI recommended in preference to SAVR (Class I, LOE A).

risk_thresholds.high_or_prohibitive_risk: TAVI recommended if post-TAVI survival >12 months with acceptable QoL (Class I, LOE A).
risk_thresholds.low_risk: Both SAVR and TAVI options; age-based decision framework applies.
risk_thresholds.prohibitive_risk_above_15_percent: Palliative care if post-procedure survival <12 months.

anatomic_criteria.BAV: TAVI may be considered (Class IIb, LOE B-NR). SAVR preferred due to asymmetric calcification.
anatomic_criteria.small_or_large_annulus: May favor SAVR for annular enlargement procedures.

age_cutoffs.under_70: SAVR recommended if surgical risk is low (STS-PROM and EuroSCORE II <4%) (Class I, LOE B).
age_cutoffs.70_and_over_tricuspid_AV: TAVI recommended if anatomy suitable for transfemoral approach (Class I, LOE A).
age_cutoffs.all_remaining_bioprosthesis_candidates: SAVR or TAVI recommended per Heart Team assessment (Class I, LOE B).

risk_thresholds.low_surgical_risk_definition: STS-PROM <4% AND EuroSCORE II <4% AND Heart Team assessment.
risk_thresholds.unsuitable_for_surgery_and_TF: Non-transfemoral TAVI should be considered (Class IIa, LOE B).

anatomic_criteria.BAV: TAVI may be considered at increased surgical risk if anatomy suitable (Class IIb, LOE B). SAVR remains primary treatment for BAV, especially if young or coexistent aortopathy.

class_I_recommendations.recommendation: AVR indicated in symptomatic severe high-gradient AS
AVR indicated in asymptomatic severe AS with LVEF <50%
AVR indicated in symptomatic low-flow low-gradient severe AS with reduced LVEF after confirmation
AVR indicated in asymptomatic severe AS undergoing other cardiac surgery
Heart Team assessment required for mode of intervention
TAVI recommended for elderly patients with suitable anatomy

ACCAHA2020.detail: >80 years or life expectancy <10y, Class I LOE A

ESC2025.detail: >=70 years with tricuspid AV and suitable anatomy, Class I LOE A

class_I_recommendations.agreement: DISAGREE on age threshold: ACC/AHA uses 80, ESC uses 70

class_I_recommendations.recommendation: SAVR recommended for younger patients

ACCAHA2020.detail: <65 years or life expectancy >20y, Class I LOE A

ESC2025.detail: <70 years if surgical risk is low, Class I LOE B

class_I_recommendations.agreement: DISAGREE on age threshold: ACC/AHA uses 65, ESC uses 70

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: AVR reasonable in asymptomatic very severe AS (Vmax >=5 m/s) with low surgical risk

ESC2025.note: Grouped with other triggers: severe calcification + rapid progression, elevated BNP, LVEF <55%

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: AVR reasonable in asymptomatic severe AS with abnormal exercise test
AVR reasonable in asymptomatic severe AS with BNP >3x normal

ESC2025.note: Listed as trigger within composite recommendation

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: Early intervention should be considered as alternative to watchful waiting in asymptomatic severe high-gradient AS with low procedural risk
class_IIa_recommendations.ACCAHA2020: Not explicitly a Class IIa recommendation in 2020 (preceded EARLY TAVR trial)

ESC2025.note: NEW recommendation based on EARLY TAVR, RECOVERY, AVATAR, EVoLVeD trials

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: Intervention should be considered in symptomatic low-flow low-gradient AS with preserved LVEF after confirmation
Non-transfemoral TAVI should be considered for inoperable patients without TF access
class_IIa_recommendations.ACCAHA2020: Not explicitly addressed as Class IIa

class_IIb_recommendations.recommendation: AVR may be considered in asymptomatic severe AS with rapid Vmax progression >=0.3 m/s/year
AVR may be considered in asymptomatic severe AS with progressive decrease in LVEF to <60%
class_IIb_recommendations.ESC2025: Incorporated as IIa trigger: LVEF <55% (upgrade and stricter threshold)

class_IIb_recommendations.recommendation: AVR may be considered in moderate AS undergoing other cardiac surgery
TAVI may be considered for BAV stenosis at increased surgical risk if anatomy suitable
Balloon aortic valvotomy may be considered as bridge

class_III_recommendations.recommendation: Palliative care recommended if post-procedure survival <12 months or minimal QoL improvement expected
class_III_recommendations.note: Not Class III per se, but explicit recommendation against intervention in futile cases

key_trials: PARTNER (high risk, balloon-expandable vs medical therapy/SAVR)
Evolut Low Risk (low risk, self-expanding vs SAVR)
DEDICATE (low risk, investigator-initiated, TAVI vs SAVR - cited only by ESC 2025)
NOTION 2 (includes BAV subgroup - cited only by ESC 2025)
EARLY TAVR (asymptomatic severe AS, TAVI vs surveillance - cited only by ESC 2025)
EVoLVeD (asymptomatic severe AS with fibrosis, early intervention vs surveillance - cited only by ESC 2025)
RECOVERY (asymptomatic very severe AS, early SAVR vs surveillance)
AVATAR (asymptomatic severe AS, early SAVR vs surveillance)

class_I_recommendations.recommendation: AV surgery recommended in symptomatic severe AR regardless of LV function
AV surgery recommended in asymptomatic severe AR with LV dysfunction
class_I_recommendations.agreement: Slight difference in LVEF threshold: ACC/AHA <=55%, ESC <=50% for Class I

class_I_recommendations.recommendation: AV surgery recommended in severe AR patients undergoing other cardiac surgery

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: AV repair should be considered in selected patients at experienced centres when durable results expected

class_IIb_recommendations.recommendation: AV surgery may be considered in asymptomatic severe AR with LVESDi >22mm/m2 or LVESVi >45mL/m2 or LVEF <=55% if low surgical risk
class_IIb_recommendations.ACCAHA2020: Partially covered by IIa recommendation with different thresholds

class_IIb_recommendations.recommendation: TAVI may be considered for severe AR in symptomatic patients ineligible for surgery if anatomy suitable
class_IIb_recommendations.ACCAHA2020: Not addressed (emerging technology at time of publication)

surgical_vs_transcatheter.summary: Surgery remains the standard for AR. TAVI for AR is only IIb in ESC 2025 for inoperable patients. ACC/AHA 2020 did not address TAVI for AR. This is a purely surgical domain in both guidelines.

class_I_recommendations.recommendation: MV intervention recommended in symptomatic severe primary MR
MV surgery recommended in asymptomatic severe primary MR with LV dysfunction (LVEF <=60% or LVESD >=40mm)
class_I_recommendations.agreement: Full agreement; ESC adds indexed threshold

class_I_recommendations.recommendation: MV repair preferred over replacement when durable result expected
Surgical MV repair recommended in low-risk asymptomatic patients with severe PMR and preserved LV function if criteria met
class_I_recommendations.ACCAHA2020: Not a Class I; listed as IIa with >95% success rate and <1% mortality

ESC2025.detail: If at least 3 of: AF, SPAP >50mmHg, LAVI >=60mL/m2, concomitant TR >=moderate

class_I_recommendations.agreement: DISAGREE: ESC 2025 upgrades early repair to Class I with specific criteria; ACC/AHA 2020 had as IIa

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: TEER should be considered in symptomatic severe PMR at high surgical risk with suitable anatomy
Early MV repair reasonable in asymptomatic severe PMR with normal LV function if >95% repair success

ESC2025.detail: Upgraded to Class I with specific criteria (see above)

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: MV surgery should be considered in asymptomatic PMR with PH (SPAP >50mmHg) or new AF

class_IIb_recommendations.recommendation: Minimally invasive MV surgery may be considered at experienced centres

class_III_recommendations.recommendation: MV replacement should not be performed for limited posterior leaflet disease unless repair attempted and failed at experienced centre

class_I_recommendations.recommendation: GDMT optimization mandatory before considering intervention
TEER recommended for symptomatic ventricular SMR with LVEF <50% on optimized GDMT meeting COAPT criteria

ESC2025.detail: Fulfilling specific clinical and echo criteria (Table 7)

class_I_recommendations.agreement: MAJOR DISAGREEMENT: ESC 2025 upgraded TEER for ventricular SMR from IIa to Class I based on COAPT 5-year data, RESHAPE-HF2, and meta-analysis

class_I_recommendations.recommendation: MV surgery recommended in severe ventricular SMR undergoing CABG

class_IIb_recommendations.recommendation: TEER may be considered for symptomatic ventricular SMR NOT meeting COAPT criteria after evaluation for LVAD/HTx
MV surgery may be considered in severe ventricular SMR without advanced HF, not suitable for TEER
MV surgery may be considered in moderate SMR undergoing CABG

COAPT_criteria_for_TEER.HF_hospitalization: At least one within previous year or elevated natriuretic peptides (BNP >=300 or NT-proBNP >=1000)

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: MV surgery + surgical AF ablation + LAAO should be considered in symptomatic severe atrial SMR on optimal medical therapy

ACCAHA2020.note: Less specific, mentioned MV surgery for annular dilation with preserved LV function

class_IIa_recommendations.agreement: ESC more specific and upgraded vs ACC/AHA

class_IIb_recommendations.recommendation: TEER may be considered in symptomatic atrial SMR not eligible for surgery
class_IIb_recommendations.ACCAHA2020: Not specifically addressed for atrial SMR

key_trials: COAPT (MitraClip vs GDMT for ventricular SMR - positive at 2 and 5 years)
MITRA-FR (MitraClip vs GDMT for ventricular SMR - neutral at 1 and 2 years)
RESHAPE-HF2 (MitraClip vs GDMT for ventricular SMR - positive for HF hospitalization reduction)
MATTERHORN (TEER vs surgery for ventricular SMR - non-inferiority, better safety with TEER)

class_I_recommendations.recommendation: PMC recommended in symptomatic patients without unfavourable characteristics
PMC recommended in symptomatic patients with contraindication or high risk for surgery
MV surgery recommended in symptomatic patients not suitable for PMC

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: PMC should be considered as initial treatment in symptomatic patients with suboptimal anatomy but no unfavourable clinical characteristics
PMC should be considered in asymptomatic patients without unfavourable characteristics if high thromboembolic risk or high risk of hemodynamic decompensation

class_IIb_recommendations.recommendation: TMVI may be considered in symptomatic patients with extensive MAC and severe MV dysfunction at experienced centres

degenerative_MS_with_MAC.note: Both guidelines acknowledge this is a high-risk population with limited options. ESC 2025 explicitly mentions dedicated TMVI devices as safer than off-label TAVI-in-MAC.

class_I_recommendations.recommendation: Heart Team evaluation recommended before intervention for severe TR
Concomitant TV surgery recommended in severe TR during left-sided valve surgery
TV surgery recommended in symptomatic severe primary TR without severe RV dysfunction or PH
class_I_recommendations.agreement: ESC upgraded from IIa to Class I

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: Concomitant TV repair should be considered in moderate TR during left-sided surgery

ACCAHA2020.detail: With annular dilation >40mm or prior RHF signs

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: TV surgery should be considered in asymptomatic severe primary TR with RV dilation/dysfunction
TV surgery should be considered in severe secondary TR with symptoms or RV dilation/dysfunction

ACCAHA2020.note: Only for annular dilation type without PH or LV disease

class_IIa_recommendations.recommendation: Transcatheter TV treatment should be considered in high-risk patients with symptomatic severe TR despite optimal medical therapy without severe RV dysfunction or pre-capillary PH

class_IIb_recommendations.recommendation: Concomitant TV repair may be considered in mild secondary TR with annular dilation (>=40mm) during left-sided surgery
class_IIb_recommendations.ACCAHA2020: Not specifically addressed at mild TR level

class_IIb_recommendations.recommendation: Isolated TV surgery may be considered after previous left-sided surgery without severe PH or RV dysfunction
class_IIb_recommendations.ESC2025: Covered within IIa for secondary TR

key_trials: TRILUMINATE Pivotal (tricuspid TEER vs medical therapy - positive for QoL, HF hospitalizations at 2y - cited only by ESC 2025)
Tri.Fr (tricuspid TEER + GDMT vs GDMT - positive composite driven by PROMs - cited only by ESC 2025)
TRISCEND II (transcatheter TV replacement vs medical therapy - positive for symptoms/QoL - cited only by ESC 2025)

surgical_vs_transcatheter.summary: ESC 2025 introduced transcatheter TR treatment as Class IIa (LOE A) for high-risk symptomatic patients. ACC/AHA 2020 did not address transcatheter TR therapy at all. This is one of the biggest advances between the two guidelines.

ESC2025.patient_preference_paramount: Both MHV and BHV are Class I recommendations based on informed patient desire

antithrombotic_therapy_post_TAVI.ESC2025_Class_I: Low-dose ASA (75-100mg/day) for 12 months after TAVI without OAC indication (Class I, LOE A)
ESC2025_Class_III: DAPT not recommended after TAVI unless clear indication (Class III, LOE A)
Routine OAC not recommended after TAVI without baseline indication (Class III, LOE A)
antithrombotic_therapy_post_TAVI.ACCAHA2020: SAPT (aspirin) or DAPT for 3-6 months, with less definitive recommendation

guideline_agreements: Symptomatic severe AS requires intervention regardless of risk level
Heart Team decision-making is mandatory for all severe VHD
MV repair preferred over replacement for primary MR
GDMT optimization required before any intervention for secondary MR
Concomitant TV surgery recommended for severe TR during left-sided surgery
Bioprosthetic valve preferred in elderly; mechanical in young patients
Palliative care appropriate when survival <12 months even with intervention
Valve-sparing aortic root replacement recommended in young patients with aortic root disease

guideline_disagreements.topic: Age threshold for TAVI as primary treatment in AS
guideline_disagreements.significance: CRITICAL: ESC expanded TAVI indication by 10 years, reflecting DEDICATE, longer follow-up data
guideline_disagreements.editorial_angle: This is the single most impactful change for TAVI volumes globally

guideline_disagreements.topic: Age threshold below which SAVR is preferred
guideline_disagreements.significance: HIGH: 5-year difference. ESC acknowledges limited RCT data <70
guideline_disagreements.editorial_angle: The 65-70 age gap is the key battleground. ESC is more conservative than ACC/AHA for this group

guideline_disagreements.significance: CRITICAL: ESC upgraded based on COAPT 5-year, RESHAPE-HF2 data
guideline_disagreements.editorial_angle: MitraClip is now a Class I therapy in Europe for properly selected patients

guideline_disagreements.significance: CRITICAL: Entirely new therapy class since 2020
guideline_disagreements.editorial_angle: TRILUMINATE and TRISCEND II created an entirely new treatment paradigm

guideline_disagreements.topic: Early intervention in asymptomatic severe AS
guideline_disagreements.ACCAHA2020: IIa for very severe AS, IIb for rapid progression
guideline_disagreements.ESC2025: IIa for all asymptomatic severe high-gradient AS with low procedural risk (based on EARLY TAVR, RECOVERY, AVATAR)
guideline_disagreements.significance: HIGH: ESC supports broader early intervention
guideline_disagreements.editorial_angle: EARLY TAVR trial fundamentally changed the watchful waiting paradigm

guideline_disagreements.topic: Surgical repair for primary MR in asymptomatic patients with preserved LV
guideline_disagreements.ACCAHA2020: Class IIa with strict criteria (>95% repair success, <1% mortality)
guideline_disagreements.ESC2025: Class I if 3+ criteria (AF, SPAP, LA dilation, TR) at experienced centres
guideline_disagreements.significance: MODERATE: ESC expanded indications for early surgery
guideline_disagreements.editorial_angle: More asymptomatic patients will be referred for surgery under ESC guidelines

guideline_disagreements.ESC2025: Class IIb for inoperable patients with suitable anatomy
guideline_disagreements.significance: LOW-MODERATE: New option for inoperable AR patients

guideline_disagreements.topic: TV surgery for symptomatic primary TR
guideline_disagreements.significance: MODERATE: ESC reflects growing recognition of TR burden

guideline_disagreements.topic: Atrial secondary MR as distinct entity
guideline_disagreements.ESC2025: Formal definition, separate management pathway, Class IIa for surgery + AF ablation + LAAO
guideline_disagreements.significance: MODERATE: ESC provides first structured framework for atrial SMR
